mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% indicates near-zero rates consistent with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS decline periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates strengthening labor market and robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 150 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136,133 thousand as of Feb 2019
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth, equity returns accelerate over 20 days due to improved earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 426,563M and HOUST at 891K units as of Feb 2019
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST growth) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint signal triggers do not outperform single-series signals by > 5% in mean return.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline signals weakening manufacturing activity and potential demand contraction. Falling INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity markets front-run deteriorating economic conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.36 as of Feb 2019, near 2017 baseline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered INDPRO decline instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) move inversely in tight labor markets. When UNRATE falls below 4.0% while PAYEMS grows, wage inflation accelerates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% as of Feb 2019 indicates slack labor market
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage pressure
failure_condition: Joint signal (low UNRATE + strong PAYEMS) does not produce negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary policy tightening risk. Equity markets discount future rate hikes, producing negative 60-day returns as real discount rates rise.
regime_hint: inflationary pressure regime with policy tightening risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered high-inflation instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture demand momentum. When GDPC1 growth accelerates and HOUST rises, equity valuations expand due to improved growth expectations, producing positive 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% annualized and HOUST month-over-month growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15,679.7B as of Q4 2018, HOUST at 891K units as of Feb 2019
failure_condition: Joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 60-day returns in triggered periods do not exceed non-triggered baseline by > 3% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity utilization. When both decline, equity markets price in stagflation risk and reduced earnings power, producing negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.3% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 50 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.36 and PAYEMS at 136,133K as of Feb 2019
regime_hint: supply contraction with labor market softening
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite joint supply weakness.
